# `/rescue` Skill — Example Interaction

This file shows a realistic example of what the `/rescue` skill should produce
when used in VS Code Copilot Chat. It is a hand-written **golden fixture** — not
generated by an LLM. Use it as a reference when evaluating actual LLM output.

---

## Example Input (what the developer types)

```
@agent-skills /rescue
I have a prototype branch `wip/notifications` — it's been open for two weeks,
has 7 commits, and I just want to get it reviewed somehow.
```

---

## Expected Output Shape

**Phase 1 (the skill must stop after this and wait):**
1. Measure the prototype — report lines, files, commits, budget status
2. Cluster changes into proposed chapters
3. Output a proposed STORY.md
4. Explicitly ask for approval before doing anything to git

**Phase 2 (only shown if the human says "yes" to Phase 1):**
5. Create story branch
6. Build each chapter one at a time

---

## Example Phase 1 Output

---

**Prototype measurement — `wip/notifications`**

| Metric | Value | Budget |
|--------|-------|--------|
| Lines changed | 847 | 300 limit |
| Files touched | 12 | 5 limit |
| Commits | 7 | — |

⚠️ Both limits exceeded. This branch needs to be split into at least 3 chapters.

---

**Clustering analysis**

I grouped the 12 changed files into 4 clusters:

| Cluster | Files | Est. lines | Semantic type |
|---------|-------|-----------|---------------|
| Schema | `db/migrations/add_notifications.ts`, `src/models/Notification.ts` | ~120 | Data model |
| Service | `src/services/NotificationService.ts`, `src/services/EmailService.ts` | ~210 | Business logic |
| API | `src/routes/notifications.ts`, `src/controllers/NotificationController.ts` | ~180 | HTTP layer |
| UI + Tests | `src/components/NotificationBell.tsx`, `src/components/NotificationList.tsx`, `tests/services/NotificationService.test.ts`, `tests/routes/notifications.test.ts`, `tests/components/NotificationBell.test.tsx` | ~337 | View + tests |

**Coupling notes:**
- Service layer imports from the Schema cluster → Schema must land first
- API layer imports from Service layer → Service must land second
- UI imports from API types → UI must land last

---

**Proposed STORY.md**

```markdown
# Story: notifications

## Motivation
Users have no way to know when something in the app requires their attention.
This adds an in-app notification system with email delivery. Extracted from
exploratory branch `wip/notifications`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Users see a notification bell badge with an unread count
- [ ] Clicking the bell shows a list of recent notifications
- [ ] Notifications are also delivered by email (opt-out available)
- [ ] Unread notifications are marked read when the list is opened

## Proposed Chapters

| # | Branch | Files | Est. lines | Rationale |
|---|--------|-------|-----------|-----------|
| 01 | `chapter/notifications/01-add-schema` | 2 | ~120 | Foundation — model and migration; everything else depends on this |
| 02 | `chapter/notifications/02-add-service` | 2 | ~210 | Business logic — create, fetch, mark-read, email delivery |
| 03 | `chapter/notifications/03-add-api` | 2 | ~180 | HTTP endpoints — depends on service layer |
| 04 | `chapter/notifications/04-add-ui` | 3 | ~200 | Bell component, list view, and all tests |

## Coupling Notes
01 → 02 → 03 → 04 (strict linear dependency chain)

## Open Questions
- Should email delivery be opt-out (default on) or opt-in (default off)?
- Are there existing email service helpers I should reuse or replace?
```

---

**Does this breakdown look right?**

Before I create any branches, please:
1. Confirm or rename the story (`notifications` → something else if needed)
2. Adjust chapters if you want to merge, split, or reorder
3. Answer the open questions
4. Reply **"yes, restructure"** to begin Phase 2

The original `wip/notifications` branch will not be touched until you confirm.

---

## Notes on What Makes This Output Good

- Phase 1 is read-only — no git commands have run
- The budget violation is flagged immediately with specific numbers
- Chapters are under the 300-line limit individually (cluster 04 needs to be verified)
- The coupling chain is explicit — reviewer knows merge order matters
- Open questions are surfaced before the human commits to the structure
- The skill waits: no branch is created without explicit human approval
